name: Implementation quality
description: Human review of a single implementation phase_execution for task completion, scope, validation, and report accuracy.

dimensions.name: Task completed
dimensions.description: Did the implementation complete the requested task?

anchors.label: Not completed
anchors.definition: Core requested work is missing or abandoned.

anchors.label: Mostly incomplete
anchors.definition: Only minor fragments of the task are present.

anchors.label: Partially completed
anchors.definition: Main path exists but material gaps remain.

anchors.label: Mostly completed
anchors.definition: Task is done with only minor unfinished edges.

anchors.label: Fully completed
anchors.definition: Requested task is complete and usable.

anchors.label: None satisfied
anchors.definition: Acceptance criteria are unmet.

anchors.label: Few satisfied
anchors.definition: Only incidental criteria appear met.

anchors.label: Some satisfied
anchors.definition: Important criteria remain open.

anchors.label: Mostly satisfied
anchors.definition: Nearly all criteria are met with minor gaps.

anchors.label: Fully satisfied
anchors.definition: All stated acceptance criteria are met with evidence.

dimensions.name: Scope adherence
dimensions.description: Did the work stay within the requested scope?

anchors.label: Severe scope break
anchors.definition: Large unrelated changes dominate the diff.

anchors.label: Major drift
anchors.definition: Significant unrequested work is present.

anchors.label: Moderate drift
anchors.definition: Some unrequested changes are present.

anchors.label: Minor drift
anchors.definition: Almost entirely in scope with small extras.

anchors.label: In scope
anchors.definition: Changes match the requested scope.

dimensions.name: Implementation appropriateness
dimensions.description: Was the technical approach appropriate for the codebase and problem?

anchors.label: Inappropriate
anchors.definition: Approach conflicts with architecture or requirements.

anchors.label: Weak
anchors.definition: Approach is fragile or poorly fitted.

anchors.label: Acceptable
anchors.definition: Works but with notable design concerns.

anchors.label: Good
anchors.definition: Sound approach with minor concerns.

anchors.label: Excellent
anchors.definition: Approach fits conventions and constraints well.

dimensions.name: Validation adequacy
dimensions.description: Was validation adequate for the change?

anchors.label: No validation
anchors.definition: No meaningful validation evidence.

anchors.label: Inadequate
anchors.definition: Validation is superficial or mismatched.

anchors.label: Partial
anchors.definition: Some relevant checks ran; gaps remain.

anchors.label: Mostly adequate
anchors.definition: Key checks covered with minor gaps.

anchors.label: Adequate
anchors.definition: Validation matches risk and change surface.

dimensions.name: Final report accuracy
dimensions.description: Did the final report accurately describe what was done and validated?

anchors.label: Inaccurate
anchors.definition: Report materially misstates outcomes.

anchors.label: Mostly inaccurate
anchors.definition: Major claims are unsupported.

anchors.label: Mixed
anchors.definition: Some accurate claims with notable omissions or errors.

anchors.label: Mostly accurate
anchors.definition: Report is largely correct with minor issues.

anchors.label: Accurate
anchors.definition: Report matches evidence and outcomes.

dimensions.name: PR clarity
dimensions.description: Is the PR clear enough for review and merge decisions?

anchors.label: Unclear
anchors.definition: PR cannot be understood or reviewed.

anchors.label: Poor
anchors.definition: PR description/diff organization is hard to follow.

anchors.label: Adequate
anchors.definition: Reviewable with effort.

anchors.label: Clear
anchors.definition: PR is easy to review with minor gaps.

anchors.label: Excellent
anchors.definition: PR intent, scope, and validation are clear.

dimensions.name: Tool use efficiency
dimensions.description: Was tool use efficient and purposeful rather than wasteful or confused?

anchors.label: Highly inefficient
anchors.definition: Tool use is chaotic or largely wasted.

anchors.label: Inefficient
anchors.definition: Many avoidable retries or irrelevant tools.

anchors.label: Mixed
anchors.definition: Useful progress with notable inefficiency.

anchors.label: Efficient
anchors.definition: Mostly purposeful tool use.

anchors.label: Highly efficient
anchors.definition: Tight, purposeful tool use with little waste.
